1
Share Audit with Barry Esposito

2
Book 30-Minute Technical Walkthrough

Present ROI Brief to CFO Sheamus Toal
Month 1: Present $6.9M–$13.8M annual uplift calculation to new CFO Sheamus Toal. Frame as first-budget-review ROI opportunity: 17x return, 90-day pilot, no displacement, proven by Shoe Carnival.

Act II
The Evidence
What we tested — screenshot by screenshot

Footwear & Accessories Retail (Off-Price / Multi-Brand) Search Scorecard

What's Working

✅ Strong Brand Intent Detection

Critical Gaps

⚠️ Zero NeuralSearch — NLP Queries Completely Fail
⚠️ No Federated Search — Content & Loyalty Invisible
⚠️ Typo Tolerance Requires User Click — Not Auto-Correcting
⚠️ Recommendations Are Brand-Siloed, Not Collaborative
⚠️ No Personalized Search Ranking

Addressing the critical gaps would position DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse as the search leader in Footwear & Accessories Retail (Off-Price / Multi-Brand).

© Algolia Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F01: Zero NeuralSearch — NLP Queries Completely Fail CRITICAL
Semantic / NLP Search Query: "gift for her under 100"
TESTED
gift for her under 100
EXPECTED
Gender=female, price≤$100, occasion=gift — should return women's shoes and accessories under $100
FOUND
3,229 results dominated by Under Armour backpacks — keyword match on 'her' in product name 'Adore Her Slip-On'. Price filter not applied. Gender intent not understood.
Natural language queries account for a growing share of search; systems that fail NLP intent return irrelevant results and lose the sale source
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia NeuralSearch understands natural language intent — gender, price constraints, occasion, and lifestyle context — returning results matching what the shopper means, not just what they typed.
Shoe Carnival → Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse Search Audit · Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F02: No Federated Search — Content & Loyalty Invisible CRITICAL
Content Commerce / Front-End UX Query: "sneak"
TESTED
sneak
EXPECTED
SAYT should surface category shortcuts, brand pages, help articles, and loyalty program info alongside products
FOUND
SAYT shows 12 query completions and 10 product thumbnails only. No category page links. No content pages. No loyalty program shortcut. Hardcoded 'return policy' redirect works but is not dynamic search.
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia Federated Search indexes multiple data sources — product catalog, help center, loyalty pages, brand editorial, store locator — and surfaces the right content type in SAYT and full results.
Shoe Carnival → Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse Search Audit · Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F03: Typo Tolerance Requires User Click — Not Auto-Correcting CRITICAL
Typo Tolerance Query: "sneackers"
TESTED
sneackers
EXPECTED
Auto-correct silently to 'sneakers' with inline 'Showing results for sneakers' message
FOUND
Shows 'YOU SEARCHED FOR sneackers' + 'DID YOU MEAN: Sneakers' button. User must click to see corrected results. On mobile, banner may be below the fold.
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia auto-corrects typos transparently — showing 'Results for sneakers' inline with zero extra clicks. Mobile users see corrected results immediately.
Shoe Carnival → Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse Search Audit · Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F04: Recommendations Are Brand-Siloed, Not Collaborative CRITICAL
Recommendations & Merchandising Query: "Birkenstock Arizona Slide Sandal PDP"
TESTED
Birkenstock Arizona Slide Sandal PDP
EXPECTED
Cross-brand FBT recommendations — e.g., Vionic insoles, Naturalizer sandals, accessories
FOUND
PDP 'Similar Styles' carousel shows only other Birkenstock variants. 3 recommendation sections all return same-brand results only. No cross-brand or cross-category collaborative filtering observed.
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia Recommend uses collaborative filtering across the full catalog — surfacing what customers who bought THIS product also bought, regardless of brand.
Shoe Carnival → Immediate conversion uplift after enabling Recommend
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse Search Audit · Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F05: No Personalized Search Ranking CRITICAL
Dynamic Facets & Personalization Query: "sneakers"
TESTED
sneakers
EXPECTED
Re-ranked results based on user size, brand history, loyalty tier — VIP Gold members see their preferences first
FOUND
6,519 sneaker results in generic rank order for all users. No evidence of personalized re-ranking. Width facet buried in panel. 'Ideas For You' carousel in empty state is UI personalization only — search results are not personalized.
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia AI Personalization re-ranks results based on each user's browsing and purchase history. VIP loyalty tier, size, width, and brand preferences feed the personalization engine.
Shoe Carnival → Up to 3.5x increase in conversions from search
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse Search Audit · Confidential
DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse · F06: Strong Brand Intent Detection POSITIVE
Intent Detection Query: "Vince Camuto"
TESTED
Vince Camuto
EXPECTED
Brand query should route to curated brand landing page
FOUND
Redirects to '/brands/vince-camuto' — full curated brand page with editorial heading, gender funnels, featured products, and 266 brand-specific results with Category filter.
ALGOLIA SOLUTION
Algolia Rules Engine extends this pattern to long-tail brand queries without dedicated pages — the merchandising team manages all rules via a visual interface without engineering dependency.
Shoe Carnival → Merchandising team productivity doubled — changes deployed in a fraction of previous time

In their own words: Strategy vs. execution

What They Told Investors
What The Audit Found
© Algolia Confidential

What they said vs. what we found

THEY SAID
"elevate our customers' omnichannel experience"
— Doug Howe, CEO DBI FY2024 Earnings Release · 2025-03
→
WE FOUND
Site search is a custom proprietary backend — no federated content, no personalized ranking, no NeuralSearch
'gift for her under 100' returns Under Armour backpacks. Help content invisible in SAYT. Zero enterprise search vendor detected via network inspection.
THEY SAID
"customer-first and product obsessed through a data-driven approach"
— Doug Howe, CEO DBI FY2024 Earnings Release · 2025-03
→
WE FOUND
No search analytics visible in UI — no trending signals, no query performance data, no click analytics
Only 'Top Rated' and 'New Arrival' badges observed. No trending products, no search query analytics, no A/B test signals in search UI. [screenshot 04]
THEY SAID
"Our top customers shop both channels, digital and physical, and they engage with our app very regularly"
— Sarah Crockett, CMO Marketing Dive, 2025 · 2025-01
→
WE FOUND
Store locator, loyalty program, and brand editorial completely invisible in search
SAYT shows products + query completions only. Help content, store locator, and loyalty program info not surfaced. [screenshot 03]

The AI Search Gap at DSW Designer Shoe Warehouse

AI Features Deployed

  • Basic keyword matching
  • Rule-based ranking

Missing AI Capabilities

  • Semantic / NLP search
  • AI personalization
  • Predictive recommendations

Every week without AI search is a week of lost discovery revenue.

Algolia Adoption Among Competitors

20%
Use Algolia

1 of 5 direct competitors use Algolia

Competitor Search Provider Monthly Traffic Bounce Rate
Zappos Amazon/AWS Internal 14.5M — Amazon-owned — unfair advantage; benchmark for UX and personalization. No enterprise search vendor available to competitors.
Macy's Custom/Internal (Monetate + Adobe Target for personalization) 68.4M — Large custom stack (148 techs). Monetate personalization advantage vs. DSW.
Famous Footwear Undetected (Phase 2 needed) 6.8M — Most similar B&M+ecommerce model to DSW. Likely parity with DSW on search.
Steve Madden Undetected 4.6M — DTC brand; lower complexity. Likely DSW on par or ahead.
Shoe Carnival Algolia ~2M — GOLDEN ANGLE: Direct off-price footwear competitor. Algolia customer. Up to 3.5x conversions from search. Same business model as DSW.

Competitors investing in search now will capture disproportionate market share.

© Algolia Confidential

Appendix F: Sources and methodology

Methodology

Phase 1 (14 research steps): Company context, tech stack (BuiltWith 7 endpoints), traffic (SimilarWeb 14 endpoints), competitor analysis, test query design, strategic angles, hiring signals, financial profile (Yahoo Finance MCP), trigger events, investor intelligence (10-K/earnings), ICP mapping. Phase 2 (browser audit): 15 screenshots captured on dsw.com testing 19 search scenarios including SAYT, typo tolerance, NLP queries, intent detection, federated search, merchandising, mobile, recommendations, and analytics. Network inspection confirmed proprietary search backend with no enterprise vendor. Phase 3 (scoring): 10 areas scored 0–10 with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity weights. Weighted formula: 54.5/14.5 = 3.8/10.
